title: Trump Seeks to Abolish Iran’s Nuclear Stockpile, a Problem He Helped Create
summary: President Trump withdrew from the Obama-era nuclear accord in 2018, saying it was the worst deal ever. But Iran responded with an enrichment spree that haunts the negotiations to this day.

title: On Iran’s Border, Cooking Oil Trade Is a Snapshot of the Country’s Struggles
summary: The war has exacerbated Iran’s economic crisis, forcing many to cross the border into Turkey to buy the most basic goods.

title: The ‘Lasting Damage’ of Pirro’s Investigation of the Federal Reserve and Powell
summary: The Trump administration’s attacks on the Federal Reserve have rattled confidence in the central bank’s ability to operate independently before a leadership transition.

title: Firm Building Trump’s Ballroom Got a Secret No-Bid Contract for a Nearby Job
summary: The National Park Service increased the value of the contract several times over and then awarded it to Maryland-based Clark Construction, in a process that experts said was highly unusual.

title: At White House Correspondents Dinner, Trump Will Dine With Reporters He’s Been Roasting
summary: President Trump has boycotted the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in previous years.

title: Under Trump, Green Card Seekers Face New Scrutiny for Views on Israel
summary: In guidance to immigration officers, the administration describes participating in pro-Palestinian protests and criticizing Israel as “overwhelmingly negative” factors.

title: The 85-Year-Old Widow Snagged by Trump’s Immigration Crackdown
summary: In her first interview since being deported, Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, the French widow of a former G.I., recounted her experience in ICE detention.

title: They Survived Torture. Why Is the Program That Helped Them Closing?
summary: Some Bellevue Hospital officials feared having to disclose patients’ immigration status. They will get care elsewhere at the hospital, administrators say.

title: Tennessee Passed a Slate of Immigration Bills. Here’s What They Do.
summary: The Republican supermajority in the Tennessee General Assembly approved a series of immigrations bills, crafted in coordination with the White House.

title: AIDS Creeps Back in Parts of Zambia, a Year After U.S. Cuts to H.I.V. Assistance
summary: A once-robust H.I.V. treatment and prevention system, credited with saving hundreds of thousands of lives, has begun to crumble.

title: New Taxes Helped Cool London’s Housing Market. Could That Happen in New York?
summary: Economists and real estate agents are calling London’s taxation of wealthy property owners a cautionary tale for New York, where leaders have endorsed a second-home tax.

title: Measles Is Back. What Comes Next Will Be Worse.
summary: Reversing the new vaccine skepticism will require a dedicated effort.

title: Trump, Iran’s Newest Hostage
summary: The president is buried in the graveyard of vanity.

title: High Lights
summary: Our lives are governed by wondrous phenomena that we don’t often stop to consider.

title: One of Two Missing USF Doctoral Students Is Found Dead, Officials Say
summary: The University of South Florida students were reported missing last week. On Saturday, prosecutors charged a roommate of one student in both their deaths.

title: Europe Prepares for a Longer War in Ukraine, With No Strategy to End It
summary: With American dealmakers wrapped up with Iran, neither Russia nor Ukraine has a clear path to victory — or toward a negotiated peace.

title: In Wine Country, Sales Are Down and Fraud Is Rampant
summary: The industry’s murky supply chain has long attracted scammers and con artists. In the words of one expert, “Wine and fraud go hand in hand.”
